acceptance_notes: Evidence shows strong boundary competition with analytic-pipeline on 7 borderline cases
All 4 assigned tasks share staged-multi-step topology but split between engineering vs analytical verification
Mean score 58.6% suggests schema captures real pattern but needs sharper boundaries
Deterministic seed correctly identifies need to make workflow assumptions explicit and state winning conditions vs analytic-pipeline
Low support size (4 tasks) means edits should be conservative - sharpen boundaries rather than expand scope

challenger_guidance.conservative: Accept if boundary sharpening against analytic-pipeline is essential and you trust the engineering-gate distinction
challenger_guidance.differentiating: Accept - this directly addresses the 7-case analytic-pipeline boundary ambiguity by making engineering constraints the decisive signal
challenger_guidance.exploratory: Accept to test whether explicit engineering verification gates improve schema discrimination on borderline pipeline tasks

expected_effect: Sharper boundary with analytic-pipeline: tasks go to engineering-composition when they need compile/test/build gates, not just pipeline execution
Reduced false positives: pure analytical pipelines without engineering verification should route to analytic-pipeline
More explicit workflow assumption: staged-multi-step topology becomes a named precondition rather than implicit
Better loss analysis: engineering constraint extraction becomes first diagnostic step
Risk: may lose some pipeline tasks if engineering gates aren't truly load-bearing - but evidence shows these are already going to analytic-pipeline

add: emphasize: extract task-specific engineering constraints (build/compile/test requirements) before procedural scaffolding
emphasize: make staged-multi-step assumption explicit in schema application logic
emphasize: articulate when engineering gates (not just output verification) are load-bearing
expected_good_fit: tasks requiring code modification with cross-file dependency tracking
expected_bad_fit: pure analytical pipelines where the pipeline output is the deliverable
expected_bad_fit: tasks with only output-contract verification, no engineering gates
hypothesized_primary_failure_modes: boundary ambiguity with analytic-pipeline when task has pipeline structure but unclear engineering constraints
exclude: Over-expanding into analytical pipeline territory without clear engineering verification gates
Adding procedural scaffolding without grounding in task-specific build/test/compile constraints
keep: diagnose -> patch/edit -> verify sequencing (core to engineering discipline)
explicit precedence and dependency handling (distinguishes from simple pipelines)
benchmark / build / test gate awareness (key differentiator from analytic-pipeline)
expected_good_fit: build fixes, migrations, code implementation tasks with compile/test/benchmark constraints
sharpen: tool wrappers / execution interfaces → tool wrappers and execution infrastructure when build/test/deployment matters (not just script orchestration)
vague reviewer-only advice → vague advice that doesn't specify which engineering gates (compile/test/deploy) to validate against

add: treating all pipeline-structured tasks as engineering tasks without identifying engineering verification gates

sharpen.new: vague advice that doesn't specify which engineering gates (compile/test/deploy/benchmark) to validate against
sharpen.old: vague reviewer-only advice without execution discipline

add: extract task-specific engineering constraints (build/compile/test/deploy requirements) before adding procedural scaffolding
make the staged-multi-step workflow assumption explicit when rendering task guidance
articulate why engineering verification gates (compile/test/benchmark) are load-bearing for this task vs pure output validation

sharpen.new: tool wrappers and execution infrastructure when build/test/deployment gates matter, not just script orchestration
sharpen.old: tool wrappers / execution interfaces / reproducible commands

add: pure analytical pipelines where the pipeline output is the final deliverable
tasks with only output-contract or result verification, no build/compile/test gates
tasks requiring code modification with cross-file dependency tracking or compilation
tasks where engineering verification gates (compile/test/benchmark) are distinct from output contract validation
boundary ambiguity with analytic-pipeline: losing tasks that have pipeline structure but lack clear engineering verification gates
